mobile_ap.summary: Apple and Broadcom's $30 billion deal marks a significant strategic shift toward custom in-house AI chip development, signaling major changes in Apple's sourcing and design strategy.

hpc_datacenter.summary: SK hynix's record $26.5 billion Nasdaq IPO debut underscores robust demand for HBM memory chips in AI systems, while Micron achieved 87% data center margins. Aggressive recruiting by SK hynix, Samsung, and TSMC in the US and Asia reflects intensifying competition for AI talent, alongside hiring bans and credential-free recruitment initiatives driven by the semiconductor boom.

custom_soc.summary: Meta begins manufacturing its in-house AI chip in September targeting 14 gigawatts of computing power, while Chinese AI developers DeepSeek and Zhipu AI join the custom chip race with their own silicon designs. Broadcom gained 5% following expanded Apple chip deals, though NVIDIA's next AI system faces delays to 2028 due to circuit board issues, and thermal management remains a critical challenge in advanced AI chip design.

foundry.summary: Rapidus plans competitive 2nm pricing around $20,000 per wafer at 2027 launch to pressure TSMC, while Samsung targets a 1.4nm Exynos SoC after three generations despite TSMC's early lead. SK hynix's Nasdaq listing success and Samsung's divergent HBM4 strategies (4nm vs SK hynix's 12nm approach) underscore intensifying foundry competition and memory-logic integration efforts for AI applications.

packaging.summary: Advanced packaging demand surges with ASE Technology's Q2 revenue hitting a record NT$191 billion driven by momentum in heterogeneous integration technologies. Samsung developed a '2.xD' packaging architecture integrating HBM, logic, and optical components to address AI power and thermal challenges, while ZEISS opened its first Asian semiconductor innovation hub in South Korea to expand packaging and photomask collaboration capabilities.
